grants.name: Federal Volunteer Grants 2025–26
grants.notes: ⚠️ 10 DAYS LEFT. EOIs through local Federal MP. $66,665 per electorate. Must have 40%+ volunteer workforce. Both orgs are volunteer-driven — strong fit.

grants.name: Local Sporting Champions (Round 4)
grants.notes: For individual coaches, officials and competitors aged 12–18 attending championships. Could support SuperNova U18 players.

grants.name: Whittlesea Community Grants 2025-26
grants.notes: NEW — North. All year round grants open until 30 April 2026. Strong multicultural community (South Morang, Mernda, Epping, Mill Park). Also has Female Inclusion in Sport Grants (co-funded with DPV Health). Contact community.grants@whittlesea.vic.gov.au or 9217 2170.

grants.name: Whitehorse Community Grants 2026
grants.notes: Eastern suburbs. Mitcham Badminton is in Whitehorse — clean venue fit for SuperNova Learn & Play. Also Partnership Grants up to $40K/yr for 4 years. Contact grants@whitehorse.vic.gov.au.

grants.name: Darebin Community Grants 2026-27
grants.notes: NEW — North. Closes 4 May 2026. Covers Preston, Reservoir, Northcote. Priority groups include CALD, women, young people. Must deliver within Darebin. Contact Darebingrants@darebin.vic.gov.au.

grants.name: City of Melbourne Connected Neighbourhoods
grants.notes: Monthly assessment rounds. Only relevant if TVS delivers programs within City of Melbourne boundaries.

grants.name: City of Melbourne — Town Halls Scheme (CUTHS)
categories: Free venue use for NFP events
grants.notes: In-kind support for NFP organisations to use Melbourne Town Halls at no cost.

grants.name: Wyndham Medium Grants – Round 2
grants.notes: Strongest council opportunity — both orgs are Wyndham-based. Co-contribution required (volunteer hours at $45/hr count). Priority: women, CALD communities.

grants.name: Wyndham Small Grants
grants.notes: Rolling applications. Quick wins for equipment or small program costs. Both orgs Wyndham-based.

grants.name: Hume Sports Aid Grants — Pathway (Batch 2)
grants.notes: NEW — North. For Hume residents aged 24 and under. Financial support for representative competition. Could support SuperNova U18 players who live in Hume. Closes 30 June 2026.

grants.name: Merri-bek Community Participation Grants (Groups)
grants.notes: NEW — North. Rolling quick-response funding for participation projects. Covers Brunswick, Coburg, Fawkner. Must benefit Merri-bek residents. Contact communitygrants@merri-bek.vic.gov.au.

closedGrants.name: Victorian Sporting Club Grants (Round 2)
closedGrants.reason: Closed 7 April 2026. All categories reopen in 2026-27 Round 1 (~Oct 2026).

closedGrants.name: Maroondah Annual Community Grants 2026/27
closedGrants.reason: Closed 2 April 2026. Up to $10K. Outcomes mid-July 2026. Enquiry sent to Josh Burt re: outside-council venue.

closedGrants.name: Darebin Older and Active Community Grant 2026-27
Hume Annual Community Grants 2026 (Round 2)
closedGrants.reason: Round 2 applications closed. 92 recipients shared $352K. Watch for 2027 round.

closedGrants.name: Merri-bek Annual Grants 2026-27
closedGrants.reason: Closed 28 February 2026. Community Strengthening (up to $7K), Facility Improvement, Organisational Establishment (up to $10K/yr for 3 yrs). Next round ~Jan 2027.

closedGrants.name: Hobsons Bay Make it Happen Grants
closedGrants.reason: Closed 30 March 2026. Up to $25K. Next round ~Feb 2027.

closedGrants.name: Melton Community Grants 2025/26
closedGrants.reason: Closed 31 March 2026. Up to $5K. Watch for 2026/27.

closedGrants.name: Knox Leisure Minor Capital Works Grant
closedGrants.reason: Closed 15 March 2026. Opens 1 Feb each year.

closedGrants.name: City of Melbourne Community Inclusion Grants 2026-27
City of Melbourne Community Events Grants 2026-27
Change Our Game Community Activation Grants 2024-25
closedGrants.reason: Closed. Up to $10K for women in sport. Watch for 2025-26 round.

closedGrants.name: Sport & Rec Vic Significant Sporting Events

upcomingGrants.name: City of Melbourne Annual Arts Grants 2027
upcomingGrants.note: Arts projects in City of Melbourne.

upcomingGrants.name: Knox Community Development Fund 2026-27
upcomingGrants.note: Eastern suburbs — ACM Bayswater is in Knox. Sport/leisure, multicultural, community strengthening. Strong fit for SuperNova eastern expansion.

upcomingGrants.name: Hume Community Grants 2027
upcomingGrants.note: North. Broadmeadows, Craigieburn, Sunbury. Strong multicultural community. Operational grants ($1.5K), Community Strengthening ($7K), 3-year grants ($10K/yr). Previously funded multicultural events and women's sport.

upcomingGrants.name: Whittlesea Female Inclusion in Sport Grants
upcomingGrants.note: North. Co-funded with DPV Health. Specifically for women's sport participation. Excellent fit for SuperNova Learn & Play — Women Only.

upcomingGrants.name: Merri-bek Annual Grants 2027-28
upcomingGrants.note: North. Brunswick, Coburg, Fawkner. Priority: migrants, refugees, women, CALD communities.

upcomingGrants.name: Change Our Game Community Activation Grants 2025-26
upcomingGrants.note: Perfect for SuperNova Women's Learn & Play. 3 streams: Participation, Education, Community Leaders.

upcomingGrants.name: Multicultural Festivals & Events Program 2025-26
upcomingGrants.note: Strong fit for TVS multicultural events (Holi, Diwali).

upcomingGrants.name: Wyndham Active Revitalisation Program
upcomingGrants.note: $3.75M program. Requires lease/licence at Council facility.

upcomingGrants.name: Sporting Club Grants Program 2026-27 (Round 1)
upcomingGrants.note: All 5 categories available again for SuperNova.

fifthark.profile: Early-stage AI software company, under $20M turnover
fifthark.grant_eligibility: Federal tech grants only (R&D Tax Incentive, Industry Growth Program). NOT eligible for sport or community grants directly.

supernova.grant_eligibility: Eligible for sport, recreation, and community grants. Receives grant funding and pays FifthArk as vendor.

meta.vendor_model: SuperNova applies for sport/community grants → receives funding → pays FifthArk invoice → FifthArk delivers the Badminton App platform. Completely legitimate and standard practice in funded community sport projects.
meta.disclaimer: Programs open and close regularly. Verify all details directly with the relevant agency before applying.

dealdesk_ai_grants.name: R&D Tax Incentive (RDTI)
dealdesk_ai_grants.action: Engage R&D Tax Incentive specialist accountant immediately. Start logging all eligible development activities from today.
relevant_activities: Novel AI classification prompts where outcome was technically uncertain
Make.com + OpenAI pipeline where behaviour was unknown in advance
Industry-specific guardrail system — no equivalent existed
OAuth and Upstash AES-256-GCM token management — new technical approach
Experimental prompt engineering — multiple approaches tested before success
warnings: Records must be kept during the year — not reconstructed retrospectively
ATO has tightened audits — document the uncertainty, not just the outcome
Grants received for R&D work reduce claimable RDTI expenditure dollar-for-dollar
Missing registration deadline means no claim — no extensions

dealdesk_ai_grants.name: Industry Growth Program — Stage 1
dealdesk_ai_grants.administered_by: Dept of Industry, Science and Resources
dealdesk_ai_grants.amount_display: Up to $25,000 in funded advisory support
dealdesk_ai_grants.action: Search Industry Growth Program on business.gov.au. Check Stage 1 eligibility for early-stage digital economy businesses.
relevant_activities: Fund a commercialisation advisor, grant writer, or growth strategist for DealDesk AI scale-up

dealdesk_ai_grants.name: LaunchVic — Victorian AI and DeepTech Programs
dealdesk_ai_grants.deadline: Subscribe to alerts — rounds close fast
dealdesk_ai_grants.amount_display: Varies — up to $400K in recent rounds
dealdesk_ai_grants.action: Register on launchvic.org and subscribe to grant alerts immediately.
relevant_activities: AI-specific founder rounds open periodically — Melbourne-based AI companies are target

dealdesk_ai_grants.name: City of Melbourne Business Growth and Impact Grants
dealdesk_ai_grants.action: Verify FifthArk's registered address is within City of Melbourne municipality. Monitor for next round.
relevant_activities: DealDesk AI qualifies as new product launch under Expansion stream
warnings: Must operate within City of Melbourne municipality — verify address first

dealdesk_ai_grants.name: Export Market Development Grant (EMDG)
dealdesk_ai_grants.deadline: Annual cycle — not relevant until first international client
dealdesk_ai_grants.action: Not relevant yet. Track export expenses from first international client (NZ, UK, Canada).
relevant_activities: International marketing
Overseas client acquisition
Export market feasibility

badminton_app_grants.framing_principle: Sport grants are never about the product. They are about the community outcome. Always frame around who benefits, how many people, what barriers are removed, what Victorian Government priorities are served.
badminton_app_grants.winning_language: This grant will remove admin barriers preventing 400+ multicultural players from accessing competitive badminton. By digitising registrations, draws, and scoring, we reduce volunteer workload significantly, enable more events, and make competitive badminton accessible to more families across Melbourne's west.

pathway_a_fifthark.name: R&D Tax Incentive — Badminton App Development
pathway_a_fifthark.amount_display: 43.5% refundable cash offset — claim alongside DealDesk AI
pathway_a_fifthark.action: Log Badminton App development activities alongside DealDesk AI. Claim both under same RDTI registration.
relevant_activities: Tournament draw generation algorithm — novel, technically uncertain
Graded team balancing system — no existing equivalent
Live scoring and real-time leaderboard synchronisation
Player registration and payment integration — new technical approach
Any feature where multiple approaches were tested before one worked

pathway_a_fifthark.name: Australian Sports Commission — The Park Innovation Hub
pathway_a_fifthark.action: Search 'The Park' at sportaus.gov.au. Register FifthArk as a sport technology innovator.
relevant_activities: Opens doors to national sporting organisations
Establishes FifthArk as credible sport tech company
Supports B2B expansion beyond SuperNova

pathway_b_supernova.name: Australian Sports Foundation — Tax-Deductible Fundraising
pathway_b_supernova.amount_display: Unlimited — community fundraising with tax deductions for donors
pathway_b_supernova.action: Register SuperNova at asf.org.au. Launch a campaign: 'Help SuperNova go digital — fund our tournament platform for 400+ players.'
relevant_activities: No competitive application — donors get tax deduction, SuperNova gets funds
Campaign: Help SuperNova go digital

pathway_b_supernova.name: Victorian Sporting Club Grants — Category 5 (Governance & Engagement)
pathway_b_supernova.status: Check portal — current round deadline was 7 Apr 2026
pathway_b_supernova.action: Check sport.vic.gov.au — is current round still open? If not, plan for 2026-27.
relevant_activities: Category 5 specifically lists digital tools for participant engagement and communication

pathway_b_supernova.name: Wyndham City Council Community Grants
pathway_b_supernova.action: Confirm SuperNova's venue is within Wyndham (Werribee, Hoppers Crossing, Point Cook, Tarneit, Truganina). Contact funding@wyndham.vic.gov.au before applying.
relevant_activities: Digitising tournament management to grow badminton participation across Wyndham
Reducing volunteer admin burden, enabling more community events
warnings: Confirm venue address is within Wyndham City boundaries before applying

pathway_b_supernova.name: Maribyrnong City Council Community Grants
pathway_b_supernova.action: Only apply here if SuperNova's venue is in Footscray, Yarraville, Seddon, or Kingsville — not if already applying to Wyndham.
relevant_activities: Alternative council grant if primary venue is in Maribyrnong not Wyndham
warnings: Apply to Wyndham OR Maribyrnong for the same project — not both

pathway_b_supernova.name: Multicultural Victoria Community Grants
pathway_b_supernova.action: Gather player demographic data. Check multiculturalvictoria.vic.gov.au for current rounds.
relevant_activities: Badminton in Melbourne's west has a significantly multicultural player base
Platform removes language and accessibility barriers for diverse communities

pathway_b_supernova.name: Badminton Victoria / Badminton Australia
pathway_b_supernova.action: Contact Badminton Victoria. Ask about technology grants for affiliated clubs and auspicing options for larger applications.
relevant_activities: Technology grants for affiliated clubs
Potential auspice arrangement for larger grant applications

priority_order.action: Engage R&D specialist — claim DealDesk AI + Badminton App development
Register with Australian Sports Foundation — launch tax-deductible fundraising campaign
Register at The Park — Australian Sports Commission
Check Sporting Club Grants Program Category 5 — apply if still open
Confirm council area — apply Wyndham or Maribyrnong community grants
Register LaunchVic — subscribe to AI grant alerts
Contact Badminton Victoria re grants and auspice options
Investigate Multicultural Victoria grants — gather player demographic data
